feedback_text:
DIAGNOSIS: 13 failing sub-attributes (61.8 pts recoverable), 0 passing. 4 regressions detected — recovery is top priority.

==================================================
REGRESSIONS (RECOVER THESE FIRST — highest priority):
==================================================

  REGRESSED: code_api_design — was 75% at iter 1, now 50% (-25%)
    What was lost: The complete docstring for the detect_delimiter function was truncated mid-sentence, cutting off at 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency an' instead of the complete 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency analysis.'
    Why it dropped: The current version appears to have been cut off or truncated during generation, resulting in an incomplete docstring for the detect_delimiter function, which reduces the overall docstring completeness score from the rubric's perspective
    → RECOVER: Complete the truncated docstring for detect_delimiter function by finishing the sentence to read 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency analysis.' and ensure all helper functions have complete docstrings matching the quality level of the main parse_csv function

  REGRESSED: code_testability — was 75% at iter 1, now 57% (-18%)
    What was lost: The complete implementation of the detect_delimiter function was truncated mid-sentence in the current version, cutting off at 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency an' instead of showing the full implementation with fallback logic
    Why it dropped: The code excerpt was cut short, making it appear that the helper functions are incomplete or missing their implementations, which reduces the perceived modularity and testability since the full modular structure cannot be evaluated
    → RECOVER: Complete the detect_delimiter function implementation and ensure all helper functions (_read_source, detect_headers, parse_rows) are fully shown with their complete implementations to demonstrate the modular design and allow proper testability assessment

  REGRESSED: code_robustness — was 75% at iter 1, now 60% (-15%)
    What was lost: The detect_delimiter function's fallback mechanism description was truncated mid-sentence from 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency analysis.' to 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency an', indicating incomplete implementation of the robust delimiter detection logic that provided multiple detection strategies.
    Why it dropped: The current version appears to have been cut off or truncated during generation, losing the complete implementation of the detect_delimiter function and likely other helper functions (_read_source, detect_headers, parse_rows) that were fully implemented in the best version. This truncation suggests the robustness mechanisms are incomplete or missing entirely.
    → RECOVER: Complete the truncated detect_delimiter function docstring and ensure all helper functions (_read_source, detect_headers, parse_rows) are fully implemented with the same comprehensive error handling, fallback mechanisms, and data integrity checks that were present in the best version. Maintain the structured error reporting and anomaly detection throughout all functions.

  REGRESSED: code_correctness — was 75% at iter 1, now 68% (-8%)
    What was lost: The fallback mechanism description and implementation details in the detect_delimiter function. The best version explicitly mentioned 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency analysis' and showed the beginning of a robust fallback implementation, while the current version's detect_delimiter function is cut off mid-sentence at 'Uses csv.Sniffer with fallback to consistency an'
    Why it dropped: The current version appears to be truncated or incomplete, cutting off the detect_delimiter function implementation before showing the fallback consistency analysis logic. This makes the delimiter detection appear less sophisticated than the best version, even though the scorer notes it has 'intelligent fallback that scores delimiter consistency across rows'
    → RECOVER: Complete the detect_delimiter function implementation by finishing the docstring and adding the full fallback consistency analysis code that was present in the best version. Ensure the function shows both the csv.Sniffer primary detection and the complete fallback mechanism that analyzes delimiter consistency across multiple rows when the sniffer fails.

    Measurement spec: Uses Python idioms and stdlib appropriately
    Evidence found: not found in content
    ⚠ This is a PENALTY violation — removing it recovers points

  FIX 6: code_correctness.edge_case_resilience (current: 50%, 3.6 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Handles standard edge cases like empty rows, column count mismatches, and encoding issues, meeting rubric requirements but without additional sophistication.
    Measurement spec: % of edge cases handled without crash
    Evidence found: Goes beyond rubric with additional edge case handling: no evidence of independent insight beyond standard edge cases
    ✗ FAILED CHECK: Goes beyond rubric with additional edge case handling
      Evidence: no evidence of independent insight beyond standard edge cases
    → ACTION: Add handling for additional edge cases like embedded newlines in quoted fields, malformed quotes, or BOM characters.

  FIX 7: code_robustness.data_integrity (current: 50%, 3.5 pts at stake)
    Scorer's critique: Maintains data integrity by reporting all anomalies and preserving data structure, meeting rubric requirements without additional sophistication.
